arguments.task: Research Plain using official first-party sources only. Find and extract evidence for: product description, developer/API docs, authentication methods for API, pricing or access requirements, whether API is public/self-serve or gated, API style (REST or GraphQL), any rate limit docs, and whether an official or notable MCP server/integration exists. Prioritize pages on plain.com, docs.plain.com or official GitHub. Return the exact URLs visited and concise findings with quotations where possible.

input.thought: Start a browser research run on official Plain sources.

raw_result.description: Customer support platform built for teams and AI agents to work in the same support threads.
raw_result.auth_notes: Plain GraphQL docs describe authentication via API keys used as Bearer tokens, associated with machine users (`Authorization: Bearer plainApiKey_xxx`).
raw_result.gating_reason: Plain pricing page shows self-serve Foundation and Horizon plans plus a 7-day free trial; Frontier is custom/contact sales.

api_surface.breadth: Public GraphQL API documented for building on Plain; docs indicate Plain itself is built on the exposed GraphQL API, but breadth of endpoint/domain coverage was not exhaustively verified from the gathered evidence.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official docs mention an MCP Server ('try our MCP Server instead') and agent-focused documentation exists.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No explicit numeric rate limits were verified in gathered evidence. Evidence suggests 429 handling exists via a documented `RateLimitError`, so limits likely exist but are not clearly published in the reviewed pages.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official GraphQL docs are public; auth is straightforward machine-user API key bearer auth; product explicitly positions itself for AI agents; pricing includes self-serve paid tiers and free trial; official MCP signal present in docs.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Rate-limit specifics, exact MCP documentation page/implementation details, and full API breadth were not directly verified beyond the gathered pages and browser-extracted notes.
